From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

British television screenwriter and playwright

| Ben Schiffer | |
| --- | --- |
| Born | (1983-07-12) 12 July 1983 (age 42)  [Amsterdam](/wiki/Amsterdam "Amsterdam"), The Netherlands |
| Alma mater | [Trinity College Dublin](/wiki/Trinity_College_Dublin "Trinity College Dublin") |
| Occupations | [Television screenwriter](/wiki/Television_screenwriter "Television screenwriter"), [playwright](/wiki/Playwright "Playwright") |
| Spouse | [Emily Taaffe](/wiki/Emily_Taaffe "Emily Taaffe")  ​ (m. 2016)​ |

**Ben Schiffer** (born 12 July 1983) is a British [television](/wiki/Television "Television") [screenwriter](/wiki/Screenwriter "Screenwriter") and [playwright](/wiki/Playwright "Playwright").

He is married to actress [Emily Taaffe](/wiki/Emily_Taaffe "Emily Taaffe").

## Early life and education

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ben_Schiffer&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Early life and education")]

Schiffer was born in [Amsterdam](/wiki/Amsterdam "Amsterdam"), the Netherlands, the second son of Jewish American emigrants. He moved to London at the age of two. He attended [University College School](/wiki/University_College_School "University College School") before moving to [Dublin](/wiki/Dublin "Dublin"), Ireland to read English and History at [Trinity College Dublin](/wiki/Trinity_College_Dublin "Trinity College Dublin"), graduating with a [First-Class Honours](/wiki/British_undergraduate_degree_classification#First-class_honours "British undergraduate degree classification") [B.A.](/wiki/Bachelor_of_Arts "Bachelor of Arts") in 2006. His first commission, whilst still at University, was the short film *Hot Or Not*, broadcast on BBC One in 2006.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Career

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ben_Schiffer&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Career")]

### Theatre

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ben_Schiffer&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Theatre")]

Schiffer wrote his first play, *Paper Tigers*, whilst still at university; the play ran to sell-out crowds at the [Edinburgh Fringe](/wiki/Edinburgh_Festival_Fringe "Edinburgh Festival Fringe").[[2]](#cite_note-2) His recent theatre work includes *His Ghostly Heart* and *50 Ways To Leave Your Lover*, both commissioned by and performed at The [Bush Theatre](/wiki/Bush_Theatre "Bush Theatre") as well as at the [Edinburgh Fringe Festival](/wiki/Edinburgh_Fringe_Festival "Edinburgh Fringe Festival"). Ben has been on the [Royal Court Theatre](/wiki/Royal_Court_Theatre "Royal Court Theatre")'s Young Writers Scheme and has had his previous plays performed in London, Dublin, Edinburgh and the Latitude Festival.[[3]](#cite_note-3)

### Television

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ben_Schiffer&action=edit&section=4 "Edit section: Television")]

Schiffer wrote episodes of *[Brief Encounters](/wiki/Brief_Encounters_(TV_series) "Brief Encounters (TV series)")* and *[Spooks: Code 9](/wiki/Spooks:_Code_9 "Spooks: Code 9")* in 2006 and 2008, respectively. He then would work on the [UK](/wiki/UK "UK") teen drama *[Skins](/wiki/Skins_(TV_series) "Skins (TV series)")*, writing a total of five episodes for the first four series, as well as contributing a story for the episode *Abbud* in 2011. In 2013, he wrote and co-executive produced *[Dates](/wiki/Dates_(TV_series) "Dates (TV series)")*, created by *Skins* creator [Bryan Elsley](/wiki/Bryan_Elsley "Bryan Elsley") for [Channel 4](/wiki/Channel_4 "Channel 4").[[4]](#cite_note-4) He wrote a pilot, *Goths*, with [Jack Thorne](/wiki/Jack_Thorne_(writer) "Jack Thorne (writer)"), for Shine Media. His previous comedy pilot *Not Safe For Work* won an RTS Student Award in 2010. He would contribute episodes to *[Stan Lee's Lucky Man](/wiki/Stan_Lee%27s_Lucky_Man "Stan Lee's Lucky Man")*, Netflix's *[Young Wallander](/wiki/Young_Wallander "Young Wallander")*, Sky's *[Intergalactic](/wiki/Intergalactic_(TV_series) "Intergalactic (TV series)")* and *[Ransom](/wiki/Ransom_(TV_series) "Ransom (TV series)")*, on which he also served as co-producer.

Most recently, he served as the Creator and Showrunner of *[The Turkish Detective](/wiki/The_Turkish_Detective "The Turkish Detective")*, an adaptation of Barbara Nadel's 'DI Ikmen' novels for [Paramount+](/wiki/Paramount%2B "Paramount+").
[[5]](#cite_note-5)

### Film

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Ben_Schiffer&action=edit&section=5 "Edit section: Film")]

He was cited as one of [Screen International](/wiki/Screen_International "Screen International")'s *Stars of Tomorrow* in 2009[[6]](#cite_note-6) and his screenplay *Cheerleaders 3D* came second in the 2010 *Brit List* with 8 Votes.
